key_insight: WarpagePINN uses a two-stage physics-informed neural network to predict chiplet temperature and thermal warpage without labeled simulation data.
engineering_relevance: It offers a fast, FEM-accurate surrogate for advanced packaging design, especially useful for evaluating CTE-mismatch-driven warpage early in the workflow.
method: Stage 1: Fourier-series temperature model trained only by the heat-equation loss and boundary-condition satisfaction.
Stage 2: MLP warpage model trained with a hybrid supervisory strategy using an energy-based loss.
Optional parametric formulation quantifies uncertainty in CTE-driven responses.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports excellent agreement with finite element results, with MAE of 0.2 μm and about 1000× speedup in CTE parameterization studies.
watch_item: As with many PINN approaches, performance may depend on careful loss balancing and can be sensitive when extending to more complex geometries or boundary conditions.
paper_title: WarpagePINN: Thermal Warpage Prediction in Advanced Packaging via a Two-Stage Physics-Informed Neural Networks
